releases.name: 1.0.0 — NextDeck

releases.author: onlymykazari
releases.description:
## Fix

Lists no longer duplicate after every sync (\"4 lists become 8, then 12\").

### Root cause

A three-way race between pull, our own file writes, and the vault event handler:

1. **Pull** replaces \`localBoard.lists\` wholesale with fresh uids where needed.
2. **pullCards** then calls \`writeCardFile\` per refreshed card (pre.22 addition), triggering vault modify events.
3. Those events hit the **250ms folder-sync debounce**, which runs \`reconcileListsFromIndex\`.
4. \`reconcileListsFromIndex\` reads the **stale** index file's list meta (pull never rewrites it), sees uids that don't exist in current \`board.lists\`, and **re-creates them as empty lists**.

Result: every sync resurrects the previous generation of list uids, so a 4-list remote board becomes 8 locally, then 12, etc.

### Fix

At the end of pull, re-run \`writeBoardIndexFile\` for every bound board, wrapped in the plugin's \`reconciling\` guard so our own writes don't fire the folder-sync debounce a second time. This keeps the on-disk list meta in lockstep with in-memory \`data.boards\`, making \`reconcileListsFromIndex\` a no-op after the vault event fires.

### Test

1. Update via BRAT to 0.5.0-pre.26
2. Sync now → the extra empty lists should collapse back to the real 4 (they were dropped in memory by pull all along; they came back only via the vault event)
3. Sync again → count stays stable at 4
releases.name: 0.5.0-pre.26 - stop list duplication on sync

releases.author: onlymykazari
releases.description: Adds a link-icon button on each attachment tile (in the card modal Attachments panel) that copies an `obsidian://open?vault=...&file=...` URL for the attachment to the clipboard, in front of the Open and Delete buttons. Handy for pasting the reference into notes.
releases.name: 0.5.0-pre.25 - copy Obsidian URL button on attachments

releases.author: onlymykazari
releases.description:
## Fix

Empty phantom boards no longer accumulate in the tab bar after each sync.

### Symptom

After every sync, extra empty boards appear next to the real ones. Card counts on the phantoms are always 0, and the number keeps growing sync-over-sync.

### Root cause

`data.boards` can end up with more than one entry that effectively "wants to be" the same remote board:
- **Same remoteId** — bindings/data.boards drifted out of sync in older versions.
- **Same folderPath** — restoreBoardsFromIndexFiles created a fresh id when the bindings map had a stale one.
- **Same name, unbound** — a leftover from user-created boards that were later linked to a remote.

The pull loop happily merges remote content onto the *first* one it finds via `findOrBindLocalBoard`, but the leftovers sit in `data.boards` and render as their own tab.

### Fix

After every pull, `pruneDuplicateBoards` runs:
1. Keep every board this sync just bound (one per remote board).
2. Drop any other board that shadows a bound one by `remoteId`, `folderPath`, or (case-insensitive) `name`.
3. Purge bindings that pointed at dropped ids so `nameMatch` doesn't resurrect them next sync.

`sync.pull.boards` now also logs `localBoards` and `bindings` counts, and `sync.prune-duplicate-boards` records which ids got dropped.

### Test

1. Update via BRAT to 0.5.0-pre.24
2. Sync → the phantom boards should disappear on this very sync
3. Sync again → count stays stable
4. Check the sync log — `sync.prune-duplicate-boards` fires once and then stays quiet
releases.name: 0.5.0-pre.24 - prune phantom boards after pull

releases.author: onlymykazari
releases.description:
## Fix

Labels (and other fields) changed on Deck's Web UI now show correctly both on the board tile **and** inside the card modal.

### Why "内外读的不是一个数据"

Yes — exactly. The plugin has two read paths:

- **Board view / card tile**: reads `plugin.data.cards[cardId]` directly from memory. Always fresh after sync.
- **Card modal**: on open, calls `hydrateCardFromFile(card)` which reads the card's `.md` file and overwrites in-memory fields (`title`, `labels`, `details`, dates, checklist) with whatever the file has.

Before pre.22, `runPull` only updated the in-memory model + `data.json` — it never rewrote the md files. So after a webui-side label change, the tile picked up the new label but the modal, on open, saw the stale md file and clobbered the freshly-synced data with the old values.

### Fix

`pullCards` now tracks every card whose model was refreshed and calls `plugin.writeCardFile(card)` for each at the end of the pull. That keeps `.md` in lockstep with `data.cards` so both read paths agree.

### Test

1. BRAT update to 0.5.0-pre.22
2. On Deck's Web UI, add a label to some card
3. Sync in Obsidian
4. Board tile shows the new label ✅
5. Click into the card → modal should also show the new label ✅ (was stale before)
releases.name: 0.5.0-pre.22 - flush markdown files after pull

releases.author: onlymykazari
releases.description:
## Fix

Labels created on Deck's web UI now show up in the Obsidian label picker after sync.

### Root cause

The label sync had two data structures with different names for the same thing:
- `localBoard.labels` — per-board catalog (uses `title` field). Filled correctly by pull.
- `data.labels` — global picker list (uses `name` field). **Never updated by sync.**

So labels you created on Deck's Web UI were pulled correctly for card assignment but never surfaced in the picker modal, giving the appearance of a truncated list.

### Fix

`pullBoard` now calls a new `mergeBoardLabelsIntoGlobal` step that promotes every board-level label into `data.labels` (dedup by case-insensitive name, adapts `title`→`name`). Emits `sync.pull.global-labels-added` in debug log so you can verify the propagation.

### Test

1. Update via BRAT to 0.5.0-pre.21
2. Create a new label on the Deck Web UI
3. Sync in Obsidian
4. Open any card → click the label button → the new label should appear in the picker
releases.name: 0.5.0-pre.21 - propagate board labels into global picker

releases.author: onlymykazari
releases.description:
## Fix

Label changes on the Deck Web UI no longer revert when you sync from an Obsidian card that has other unsynced edits.

### Root cause

`mergeRemoteCardOntoLocal` used a coarse "if not dirty, take remote" guard, which meant that when a card was dirty on **any** field (say, you edited a checklist item locally), pull would keep the **entire** stale local record — labels included. Then `pushCardLabels` would reconcile against that stale set: assign whatever labels local had, and unassign anything on the remote that wasn't in local, wiping out the webui change.

### Fix

Merge now does a per-field decision for labels using the baseline signature stored on `card.baseline`:

- **Local labels still match baseline** → labels weren't touched locally → adopt remote
- **Local labels diverge from baseline** → true edit on both sides → keep local (existing behavior)

Other fields (title, details, etc.) still use the coarse "keep local if dirty" rule; per-field 3-way merges for them go through the existing conflict-modal path.

### Test

1. Update via BRAT to 0.5.0-pre.20
2. Make an edit locally in Obsidian on some card (say, tick a checklist item) — don't sync yet
3. On the Deck Web UI, change that card's labels
4. Sync in Obsidian — labels should reflect the webui change, and your local checklist edit should also survive

26 unit tests pass, including 2 new regression cases for this exact scenario.
releases.name: 0.5.0-pre.20 - fix label revert when card is dirty

releases.author: onlymykazari
releases.description:
## Fix

Attachments created on Deck Web UI now sync down to the local vault.

### Root cause

pre.16 wired up the OCS **list** and **upload** endpoints correctly, but the **download** step was still using the legacy `/index.php/apps/deck/cards/{cardId}/attachment/{attachmentId}` route. That route only serves pre-1.3.0 `type=deck_file` attachments. For the modern `type=file` attachments (stored in your Nextcloud Files under `/Deck/…`), it responds with 403 — hence the flood of "attachment-download-failed (403)" entries in the sync log and the empty local `attachments/<cardId>/` folders.

### Fix

`downloadAttachment` now uses the `extendedData.path` field from the OCS attachment record and streams the file directly via WebDAV:

```
GET /remote.php/dav/files/{username}/{extendedData.path}
```

which is how the Files app itself serves the bytes. Legacy `type=deck_file` attachments (very old servers) still work via the old route as a fallback.

### Test

1. Update via BRAT to 0.5.0-pre.19
2. Sync — the "attachment-download-failed (403)" entries should stop appearing in the log
3. Open a card that has attachments on Deck's Web UI → the Attachments panel should list them with thumbnails
4. Check `<board>/attachments/<cardId>/` in the vault — files should be present
releases.name: 0.5.0-pre.19 - download attachments via WebDAV

releases.author: onlymykazari
releases.description: BRAT test: Copy vault embed link button on each attachment tile — copies `![[<filePath>]]` so users can paste it directly into notes.
releases.name: feature-copy-url

releases.author: onlymykazari
releases.description:
## New: Attachments panel

The card modal now has a dedicated **Attachments** section below the description. Closes the last gap from the pre.16 attachment rework — you can finally see and manage attachments without diving into the file browser.

### What you can do

- **See every attachment** on a card, including ones that only exist on Deck (pulled down but not yet referenced by a `![[…]]` in the description)
- **Preview thumbnails** for image attachments (loaded via Obsidian's native `getResourcePath`)
- **Open** an attachment in a new pane
- **Delete** an attachment (confirms first; moves the local file to trash and the reap logic enqueues the remote deletion for the next sync)
- **Add attachment** button in the header → OS file picker → files are saved under `<board>/attachments/<cardId>/` and a `![[…]]` embed is inserted at the caret

### Behavior notes

- Files that exist locally but haven't been uploaded yet (e.g. a screenshot you just pasted) show up with a dashed border and a "pending upload" label. Next sync will register them with Deck.
- No sync-layer changes; if you're not using Nextcloud Deck sync, the panel still works as a plain local file browser for the per-card folder.
- Empty state: shows "No attachments" instead of an empty grid.

### Test

1. Update via BRAT to 0.5.0-pre.18
2. Open any card with images in the description — the panel should list them with thumbnails
3. Click "+" to add a file — verify it appears with a "pending upload" tag, and a `![[…]]` shows up at the caret
4. Sync — the tag should disappear on next open
5. Click the trash icon on any tile — the file moves to trash, sync propagates the delete
releases.name: 0.5.0-pre.18 - Attachments panel in card modal

releases.author: onlymykazari
releases.description:
## Fix

Label sync no longer fails with 400 when the board already has a label of the same name.

### Root cause

`pullBoard` was using the slim board object from `GET /boards` (Deck's list endpoint), which on some deployments does not populate the `labels[]` array. Without the catalog, `pushCardLabels` treated existing labels as new and issued `POST /boards/{id}/labels` — Deck rejected it with 400 "duplicate title".

### Fix

1. **Hydrate**: `pullBoard` now calls `GET /boards/{id}` before reconciling, guaranteeing `remoteBoard.labels` is populated.
2. **Recover**: If `createLabel` still hits 400 (edge case: label added on Deck between our pull and push), we now refetch the board's labels and match by title, reusing the existing label id instead of giving up. Logged as `labels.recovered-existing` in debug output.

### Test

1. BRAT update to 0.5.0-pre.17
2. Sync now — labels should now propagate to the card on Deck without "labels.create-failed" entries in the log
3. Toggle a label on a card in Obsidian → sync → verify on Deck Web UI
releases.name: 0.5.0-pre.17 - fix label duplicate-title 400

releases.author: onlymykazari
releases.description:
## Attachment rework (finally)

### What was wrong

The plugin was calling the wrong Deck endpoint for attachments (`/index.php/apps/deck/api/v1.0/boards/…/stacks/…/cards/…/attachments` + `type=deck_file`). On modern Deck deployments this falls through to a generic file uploader — the file gets stored in Nextcloud Files but is **not registered on the card**. That's why the attachment panel stayed empty and images loading in the description spun forever.

### What's fixed

**OCS attachment API**. Verified against the HAR of the Web UI:
- `GET /ocs/v2.php/apps/deck/api/v1.0/cards/{id}/attachments?boardId={b}`
- `POST /ocs/v2.php/apps/deck/api/v1.0/cards/{id}/attachment?boardId={b}` with `type=file`
- `DELETE /ocs/v2.php/apps/deck/api/v1.0/cards/{id}/attachments/{aid}?boardId={b}`

**Safe bidirectional wikilink rewrite** (the correct form of what pre.10–pre.14 got wrong):
- **Push**: `![[<vault-path>]]` → `[filename](https://<server>/f/<fileid> (preview))` — only when the target resolves to a freshly uploaded attachment (by filePath / basename **and** fileid). Deck's Markdown renderer will show a thumbnail.
- **Pull**: `[caption](https://<server>/f/<fileid> (preview))` → `![[<vault-path>]]` — only when fileid matches something in `card.attachments[]`. Obsidian renders inline.
- **External URLs and unresolved wikilinks are left untouched.** The plugin will never again silently replace `![[https://cdn/foo.png]]` with `foo.png`.

**Local paths reorganised.** Pasted / dropped screenshots now go to `<board>/attachments/<cardId>/` instead of the flat `<board>/attachments/` so attachment-sync's per-card scan actually finds them.

### Test

1. Update via BRAT to 0.5.0-pre.16
2. In a card, paste a screenshot — should save under `<board>/attachments/<cardId>/foo.png` and insert `![[…]]`
3. Sync now — verify in Deck that the attachment panel shows the file **and** the description renders the inline preview (should not spin)
4. External URLs (`![[https://cdn.example.com/logo.png]]`) should round-trip untouched
5. Delete an attachment on either side and sync — the other side should catch up

24 unit tests pass covering both directions of the rewrite, external URLs, unresolved wikilinks, and cross-server links.
releases.name: 0.5.0-pre.16 - attachments via OCS API + bidirectional wikilink rewrite

releases.author: onlymykazari
releases.description:
## New

- **Configurable board root folder.** Settings -> Nextcloud sync now has a **Board root folder** text field. Every board freshly pulled from Nextcloud is placed under this folder. Default: `Deck`. Empty string means "place boards directly at the vault root". Trimmed slashes are handled.
- **Move existing boards button.** Migrates every already-synced board (folder + cards + attachments) under the current Board root folder. Card file paths and attachment paths are rewritten in-place, so Obsidian links and Deck-side references stay consistent. Idempotent; name clashes get " 2" / " 3" suffixes.

## Fix

- **Explicit path recovery instead of silent assumption.** In pre.8 writeCardFile silently invented a filePath under a hard-coded folder — bad idea if the vault already reserves that name (macOS Notes.app users, etc.). Now:
  - When the card's board is resolvable, we still generate a fallback path (using the configured root), but the recovery is logged as writeCardFile.assign-path.
  - When the board can't be resolved, we throw with a readable message ("Card has no board folder assigned. Reload after the next Nextcloud sync.") instead of writing into the vault root.

## Answer to the earlier question

Attachment location is **entirely plugin-controlled**, not Deck-server-decided. Pattern: `<rootFolder>/<boardName>/attachments/<cardId>/<filename>`. Change rootFolder and hit Move now to move existing files.

## Migrate

1. BRAT -> update to 0.5.0-pre.9.
2. Settings -> Nextcloud sync -> **Board root folder**: type `Deck` (default) or your preferred path.
3. Click **Move now**.
4. Sync now.
releases.name: 0.5.0-pre.9 - configurable board root folder

releases.author: onlymykazari
releases.description:
## Fix

- **Fix EISDIR: illegal operation on a directory** when saving a card that was pulled from Nextcloud but had never been written to the vault. Root cause: pullCards + mergeRemoteCardOntoLocal keep filePath as empty string, so vault.adapter.write("") targeted the vault root (a directory) and Node threw EISDIR. The save failed, and every subsequent Nextcloud push therefore shipped the stale card content (this is why the checklist kept rendering as 1 / : on Nextcloud). writeCardFile now materializes a filePath via nextCardPath when the card has none.

## Verify

1. BRAT -> update to 0.5.0-pre.8.
2. Open the card that used to fail. Modify the checklist.
3. Save should succeed silently. The card note now lives in <board>/cards/<title>.md.
4. Sync now.
5. Nextcloud should render the correct checklist.

If Debug logging is on, look for events writeCardFile.assign-path (first save after a pull) and sync.push.update.request with a descriptionPreview that contains ## Checklist.
releases.name: 0.5.0-pre.8 - fix EISDIR on pulled cards

releases.author: onlymykazari
releases.description:
## Fixes

- **Fix Could not save card: File already exists.** writeCardFile relied on Obsidian's TFile cache which is occasionally stale (case-folded paths on macOS, race with external file replacement, sync reload). The fallback tried vault.create, which then threw. Now we probe via vault.adapter.exists + adapter.write, and a second-chance catch converts a stray already-exists error into an overwrite. The card note is always updated.
- **Fix stale checklist round-trip.** The failed save was leaving the card's Markdown file with its prior content, so every subsequent Nextcloud push shipped the old checklist entries. That's why Deck kept rendering "1" / ":" instead of the real "test" / "222test" items. With the save fix, the next sync uploads the correct checklist.

## New: Debug logging

- New Settings toggle: Debug logging (off by default).
- When on, verbose diagnostics land in the sync log and the developer console (Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+I -> Console) — file paths, sync payload shapes, checklist lengths, description previews (200 chars), and full error stacks. When off, zero overhead.
- Reproducer flow: enable Debug logging -> reproduce the bug -> Settings -> Sync log -> View sync log -> Copy diagnostics -> paste to bug report.

## Verify

1. BRAT -> update to 0.5.0-pre.7.
2. Open a card with the checklist that was showing "1" / ":" on Nextcloud.
3. Fix the checklist locally, save.
4. Sync now.
5. Nextcloud should now show the correct items.
releases.name: 0.5.0-pre.7 - fix save + debug logging

releases.author: onlymykazari
releases.description:
## Change

- **Merge the two Sync now buttons.** Settings previously showed both Sync card notes (local folder scan, inherited from upstream) and Sync with Nextcloud (two-way sync). The former is now folded into the latter — a Nextcloud sync run absorbs any orphan Markdown cards in board folders before pushing to Nextcloud. The Sync card notes row is removed.
releases.name: 0.5.0-pre.6 - single Sync now button

releases.author: onlymykazari
releases.description:
## Changes

- **Remove upstream donation entries.** The Support development button in Settings and the Support / Support developer buttons on the board toolbar + welcome view are gone, along with the underlying DONATION_URL constant. Fork users are no longer silently redirected to the upstream author's Buy Me a Coffee page. Attribution to the upstream project stays in README.md.
- **Real save-card errors.** The generic "Could not save card." toast now includes the underlying error message and is also logged into the sync log (event: save-card-failed). If saving fails again, open Settings -> Sync log -> View sync log and hit Copy diagnostics — the detailed message will be visible.

## How to find the sync log

1. Open Obsidian Settings.
2. Left sidebar -> Community plugins -> Obsidian Nextcloud Deck (or scroll the settings body to the plugin's section).
3. Scroll to the bottom of the plugin section. The last row is Sync log with a View sync log button.
4. Copy diagnostics puts a redacted JSON dump on the clipboard.

## Please share the actual error

If a checklist save still fails after installing 0.5.0-pre.5, the new toast message will contain the concrete reason (e.g. path issue, network error, etc). Please paste it here or share diagnostics.
releases.name: 0.5.0-pre.5 - drop donation button, surface save errors

releases.author: onlymykazari
releases.description:
## Fix

Description is now shipped to Deck **verbatim** — no more wikilink rewriting.

### Why the rewrite was harmful

Since pre.10 the plugin ran a helper (rewriteWikilinksForDeck) that tried to translate `![[…]]` into either a Deck-internal image URL or a plaintext filename caption before push. This was wrong for two reasons:

1. Deck's Markdown renderer refuses same-origin `/index.php/…` URLs (CSP), so the URL form displayed as plaintext anyway.
2. The pre.11+ fallback did filename-basename matching, which meant a wikilink like `![[https://cdn/.../foo.png]]` was reduced to `foo.png` on push. That mangled text then flowed back to Obsidian on the next pull, permanently corrupting the local description.

That is exactly the "云端 description 图片位置变成图片名字" behavior you reported.

### What now happens

Push no longer touches `payload.description`. Deck may display raw `![[…]]` as literal text (its Markdown renderer doesn't know the syntax) but at least the plugin isn't silently editing your source. Attachment handling is being redesigned properly through the OCS attachment API — see `.trae/documents/attachment-rework-plan.md`.

### Test

Put `![[https://cdn-g.apeuni.com/.../header_logo_s.1a7e63c6.png]]` in a card description, sync, and confirm Nextcloud shows the same raw text (not just the filename). Then you can proceed with your image-host-style experiment.
releases.name: 0.5.0-pre.15 - stop rewriting description wikilinks

releases.author: onlymykazari
releases.description:
## Fixes (in-priority order from previous conversation)

### 1. Reap loop cleaned up
reapDeletions used to keep retrying forever anything that failed with a non-404. The log you shared showed four entries stuck in an endless reap-failed retry loop, each returning 403. Root cause: Deck returns 403 (Permission denied) when a card has been soft-deleted on the web UI — the record is trashed but not gone, so DELETE never succeeds. Retrying them on every sync is what let those old remoteIds re-appear during pulls (creeping duplicates).

Fix: 404, 403, and 410 all count as "already in the terminal state we wanted" and are dropped from pendingDeletions. Added reap.treat-as-gone debug event so we can see when this fires. Same treatment applied to attachment reap.

### 2. Only one background sync timer now
The plugin was running BOTH the legacy scheduleNextcloudSync (30-60s, ignored settings) AND the new pre.13 auto-sync (respects settings). They didn't actually collide thanks to the in-flight guard, but were doing the same work twice and the legacy one bypassed the "Automatic sync" toggle. Removed the legacy scheduler entirely. The user-facing toggle is now the single source of truth.

### Note on the "duplicate card that disappears" flicker
From the log this is almost certainly Deck's web UI momentarily showing a soft-deleted card before its next state refresh. It's not the plugin creating a real duplicate — pull correctly consolidates via remoteId (see the pull.dedupe-local event). If you keep seeing it locally in Obsidian (not on Nextcloud), let me know and I'll dig further.
releases.name: 0.5.0-pre.14 - fix reap loop; single sync timer

releases.author: onlymykazari
releases.description:
## Fixes

### 1. Labels now round-trip
Deck's card update endpoint doesn't accept a labels field (see docs — only title / description / type / order / duedate). So merely writing card.labels and PUTing the card silently no-op'd. pre.13 implements the correct flow using Deck's dedicated /assignLabel and /removeLabel endpoints:
- board label catalog is cached locally and refreshed on every pull
- new local labels are auto-created on Deck if missing
- adds/removes are diffed against the current server state per push

### 2. startDate is a client-only field (confirmed against docs)
Deck's REST API only defines duedate — startDate has no upstream representation. It stays as an Obsidian-only field. Not a bug in the plugin; a limitation of Deck itself. If Nextcloud ever adds startDate to the schema I'll wire it up.

### 3. Attachment upload diagnostics
Enable Debug logging (Settings → Debug logging) and run Sync now. Every attachment upload now emits push.scan / push.upload.request / push.upload.response events showing bytes sent + all response keys. If Deck returns 2xx but no attachment id, a new attachment-upload-empty-response sync log entry appears. This should tell us within one sync why uploads are failing on your instance.

### 4. UX
- **Ribbon icon**: after signing in, a refresh-cw icon appears in the left ribbon — one click syncs. No more digging into Settings.
- **Automatic sync**: Settings → Nextcloud sync → "Automatic sync" toggle + "Sync interval (minutes)". Off by default. Clamped to >=1 min. Skipped when the OS reports offline. Never overlaps an in-progress sync.
releases.name: 0.5.0-pre.13 - label sync + ribbon sync + auto-sync

releases.author: onlymykazari
releases.description:
## Fix

- **Attachments are now registered against the card**. Root cause of the "file appears in Nextcloud files but card attachment panel is empty" symptom: uploadAttachment / deleteAttachment used the singular URL (`/cards/{id}/attachment`) but Deck's REST API is spelled in the plural form (`/cards/{id}/attachments`). On some Nextcloud deployments the singular URL falls through to a generic file upload route — the bytes get stored in the user's Files without creating the Deck attachment record. Fixed both endpoints. New uploads should now show up in the card's attachment panel.

## Existing uploads

The files sitting in your Nextcloud Files tree from earlier attempts are stray — they are not tied to any card. Recommended:
1. In Nextcloud Files, navigate to `.deck/` (or wherever previous uploads landed) and remove them, OR
2. Ignore them; they don't affect anything in Deck or Obsidian.

## Diagnostics

Debug log now includes `titlePreview` alongside `descriptionPreview` in push events, so we can see what title + description we're actually sending to Deck. If you still see the card name appearing in the description on Deck after this update, please enable Debug logging and share the diagnostics — I'd like to compare what the plugin sends vs what Deck stores.
releases.name: 0.5.0-pre.12 - fix: attachment endpoint used singular path

releases.author: onlymykazari
releases.description:
## Fixes

### 1. Attachment display on Deck (correct approach)
You were right: Deck's attachments are a first-class card module, NOT embedded through the description. Deck's Web UI renders them in a dedicated attachment panel; the description renderer does not resolve `![[…]]`, and it refuses `![](/index.php/apps/deck/cards/…/attachment/…)` because of CSP — which is why pre.10 turned into plain-text link literals.

pre.11 does the right thing:
- Wikilinks that match an uploaded attachment are removed from the description entirely. The file still shows up in Deck's attachment panel — nothing needs to be duplicated in the body.
- Wikilinks that cannot be resolved (references to other notes, unsynced files) are replaced with just their caption text — no brackets, no broken URL. Deck reads it as normal prose.

### 2. Creeping duplicate cards
Root cause: pullCards keyed the "match my local card to this remote card" lookup on remoteId, but data.cards can end up with two entries for the same remoteId (create-then-pull race, filePath-triggered re-import, etc.). Each subsequent pull would build the lookup from only one of them, leaving the other one dangling — which the next sync then treated as new and pushed as a fresh create. Multiply that by every tick and you get the exponential mess you saw.

Fixes:
- Coerce remoteId to Number when keying the map (guards against string-vs-number drift).
- On every pull, scan data.cards for duplicate remoteIds on this board, keep the first, drop the extras. Emits pull.dedupe-local into the sync log so you can see what was consolidated.
- Preserve filePath from an existing local card when merging, so writeCardFile updates the same note instead of minting a new path.

### 3. Diagnostics dump was hiding evidence
sync-log-modal.copyDiagnostics used to keep only 6 fields per event, so I couldn't see remoteId, count, descriptionPreview, matched flags, etc. Switched to a blacklist (password / appPassword / authorization) — the copy now preserves every diagnostic field so we can actually triage bugs.

## What to do

1. BRAT -> update to 0.5.0-pre.11.
2. Before syncing: clean up the duplicates on Nextcloud manually (delete extras). Local data.cards will auto-dedupe on the next pull.
3. Sync now — you should see pull.dedupe-local events in the log.
4. Verify attachment: the image is visible in Deck's attachment panel (below the description), not inline.
releases.name: 0.5.0-pre.11 - attachments off description; dedupe pull; full diagnostics

releases.author: onlymykazari
releases.description:
## Fixes

- **Checklist textbox is now full width.** The row grid reserved a 26px assignee column that is never populated in this fork, so the input was crammed into the 20px checkbox slot and appeared as a one-character-wide box. Column dropped; input now stretches to fill the row.
- **Fix Nextcloud image tile spinner.** Deck's Markdown renderer is plain CommonMark and does not understand Obsidian wikilinks (`![[foo/bar.png]]`), so referenced attachments rendered as a perpetually loading tile. On push we now rewrite each embed into `![filename](/index.php/apps/deck/cards/<cardId>/attachment/<attachmentId>)` — Deck's own attachment URL — and the image renders. Unresolvable wikilinks (references without a matching uploaded attachment) become plain markdown links so users at least see the filename instead of a broken embed. Attachment upload is reordered to run before the description update, so remote ids are known when we rewrite.

## Notes

- Pull-side conversion (rewriting the CommonMark link back to `![[…]]` when syncing Deck edits into Obsidian) will land in a later release. For now, if Deck sends back a card with the CommonMark form, Obsidian shows the markdown link — usable but not an inline embed. Editing the card in Obsidian and syncing back re-embeds properly.
releases.name: 0.5.0-pre.10 - checklist UI + wikilink rewrite for Deck

releases.author: onlymykazari
releases.description:
## Fix

- **Checklist round-trip through Nextcloud Deck.**
  Deck cards have no dedicated checklist field — items live in the description. Earlier pre-releases dropped local checklist entries on push, and Deck's UI then rendered a broken pseudo-checklist derived from unrelated bullets. This build embeds the checklist as a "## Checklist" fenced section at the end of the description on push, and strips it back into card.checklist on pull, so both sides show the same content.

## Verify

1. Update via BRAT to 0.5.0-pre.4.
2. In Obsidian, open a synced card and add checklist items.
3. Sync now.
4. On Nextcloud, the checklist should now display correctly and remain editable there too — subsequent pulls will keep both sides in sync.

## Also

- Added 5 unit tests covering the split / merge helpers and round-trip.

If you still see duplicated cards, please open **Settings → Sync log → View sync log** and hit **Copy diagnostics**, then share the JSON — the events will show whether the duplicates came from a repeated create or from a truly separate remote card.
releases.name: 0.5.0-pre.4 - fix: checklist sync

releases.author: onlymykazari
releases.description:
BRAT preview closing out **M5 (mobile prep, diagnostics, docs, tests)**.

## New in 0.5.0-pre.3 (adds on top of pre.2)

- **Sync log viewer**: Settings → Sync log → View sync log. Rolling ring buffer of the last ~200 events; error rows highlighted; **Copy diagnostics** puts a redacted JSON summary on the clipboard for bug reports (no App Password, no card bodies, server URL redacted to host only).
- **Commands**: two new command-palette entries — **Sync with Nextcloud Deck** and **View Nextcloud sync log**.
- **Unit tests**: node scripts/test-sync-units.js runs 11 red/green cases against the pure sync helpers (conflict + sync-mapper), no external deps.
- **README**: full rewrite for shipping — BRAT install, commands list, conflict policy explainer, attachment toggle notes, sync log UX, privacy / security section, compatibility matrix, dev + test docs.

## Everything from pre.2 still applies

- Two-way sync (M3): Pull -> Push -> Reap; field-level 3-way diff with prompt / local / remote / newer-wins policies; per-card ConflictModal.
- Attachment sync (M4, experimental, off by default): download / upload / reap via Deck attachment API; storage entirely inside Nextcloud (type=deck_file).
- Login Flow v2 + App Password fallback; App Password AES-GCM encrypted at rest.

## Install via BRAT

1. Open BRAT settings.
2. Add beta plugin: onlymykazari/obsidian-nextcloud-deck
3. Choose version 0.5.0-pre.3.

## Known limits

- Assignees UI hidden by design (data preserved).
- Attachments are single-request uploads; very large files may need chunked upload (later release).
- Mock server does not simulate attachments; test M4 on a real Deck server.
- Mobile: works via requestUrl but has only been smoke-tested; please report issues via the sync log's Copy diagnostics.
releases.name: 0.5.0-pre.3 - M5: sync log, commands, tests, docs

releases.author: onlymykazari
releases.description:
BRAT preview covering **M3 (two-way sync)** and **M4 (attachment sync, experimental)**.

## What is new since 0.4.0

### M3: Two-way sync
- `Sync now` button (formerly `Pull now`) runs a full **Pull -> Push -> Reap** cycle.
- Local edits mark cards `localDirty`; the manager pushes them to Nextcloud on the next tick.
- 3-way field diff (`title`, `description`, `completed`, `dueDate`, `startDate`) with baseline snapshot.
- Conflict policies: `prompt` (default) / `local` / `remote` / `newer-wins`.
- `ConflictModal`: per-field Keep local / Use Nextcloud picker; cancel skips the card.
- Local card deletions enqueue a tombstone, drained during the reap phase.

### M4: Attachment sync (experimental, off by default)
- New setting **Sync attachments (experimental)** under Nextcloud sync.
- Storage: `<boardFolder>/attachments/<cardId>/<filename>` in the vault, Deck API on the wire.
- Uses `type=deck_file` attachments; storage backend is fully managed by Nextcloud (local disk / S3 / Swift / ...).
- Pull: downloads new / modified attachments, cleans up orphans removed on Nextcloud.
- Push: uploads any files you drop into `attachments/<cardId>/` that lack a `remoteId`.
- Reap: local deletes and renames flow to Nextcloud through a tombstone queue.
- Requires a real Nextcloud + Deck server; the bundled mock does not implement attachment endpoints.

## Install via BRAT

1. Open BRAT settings.
2. Add beta plugin: `onlymykazari/obsidian-nextcloud-deck`.
3. Choose version `0.5.0-pre.2`.

## Known limits

- Assignees UI intentionally hidden (data preserved).
- No mobile-specific hardening yet (M5).
- Attachments are single-request uploads: very large files may time out; Nextcloud does its own internal chunking after receipt.
- Mock server does not simulate attachments; test on a real Deck server.

Feedback via GitHub issues.
releases.name: 0.5.0-pre.2 - Two-way sync + experimental attachments

releases.author: onlymykazari
releases.description:
First BRAT preview of Obsidian Nextcloud Deck (forked from ismailivanov/task-deck).

## What is in this build

- Plugin renamed to `obsidian-nextcloud-deck` with new VIEW_TYPE.
- Sync Deck / Relay / presence code removed. Assignee UI hidden (data preserved).
- New settings section **Nextcloud sync**:
  - Server URL input.
  - **Sign in with browser** (Login Flow v2) + **Paste App Password** fallback.
  - **Test connection** and **Sign out** (revokes the App Password server-side).
  - **Sync interval** (30s / 1m / 5m / 15m / manual) and **Conflict resolution** dropdowns.
- Read-only pull (M2): fetches boards, stacks, cards, and labels, materialises Markdown notes under `Nextcloud Deck/<board>/`.
  - Preserves local card ids and file paths across pulls.
  - Uses `ETag` / `If-None-Match` and Deck's embedded card payloads.
  - Cards that disappear on Nextcloud are removed locally (source of truth = Deck for tracked cards).
- Credentials stored via Web Crypto AES-GCM 256 with PBKDF2-SHA256 (210k iterations). Passphrase in localStorage, ciphertext in data.json.
- Local Nextcloud + Deck API mock at scripts/mock-nextcloud.js for offline development (see scripts/README-mock.md).

## Known limits

- Local edits do **not** push to Nextcloud yet (writes land in M3).
- Attachments not synced yet (M4).
- Mobile only validated in M5.
- Assignees hidden in UI; data preserved for future team support.

## Install with BRAT

1. Open BRAT settings.
2. Add beta plugin: onlymykazari/obsidian-nextcloud-deck
3. Choose version 0.4.0.

Feedback welcome via GitHub issues.
releases.name: 0.4.0 - Nextcloud Deck sync (M1+M2 preview)
